heading: Definitions
label: 30309 Definitions
notes: CodificationSection was formerly classified to , The Public Health and Welfare, prior to editorial reclassification and renumbering as this section.section 15609 of Title 42

children.heading: Carnal knowledge
children.label: 1 Carnal knowledge
children.text: The term “carnal knowledge” means contact between the penis and the vulva or the penis and the anus, including penetration of any sort, however slight.

children.heading: Inmate
children.label: 2 Inmate
children.text: The term “inmate” means any person incarcerated or detained in any facility who is accused of, convicted of, sentenced for, or adjudicated delinquent for, violations of criminal law or the terms and conditions of parole, probation, pretrial release, or diversionary program.

children.label: A
children.text: persons pending adjudication of criminal charges; or

children.label: B
children.text: persons committed to confinement after adjudication of criminal charges for sentences of 1 year or less.

children.heading: HIV
children.label: 4 HIV
children.text: The term “HIV” means the human immunodeficiency virus.

children.heading: Oral sodomy
children.label: 5 Oral sodomy
children.text: The term “oral sodomy” means contact between the mouth and the penis, the mouth and the vulva, or the mouth and the anus.

children.label: A
children.text: inmates pending bail or transport to jail;

children.label: B
children.text: inebriates until ready for release; or

children.label: C
children.text: juveniles pending parental custody or shelter placement.

children.label: A
children.text: any local jail or police lockup; and

children.label: B
children.text: any juvenile facility used for the custody or care of juvenile inmates.

children.heading: Prison rape
children.label: 8 Prison rape
children.text: The term “prison rape” includes the rape of an inmate in the actual or constructive control of prison officials.

children.label: A
children.text: the carnal knowledge, oral sodomy, sexual assault with an object, or sexual fondling of a person, forcibly or against that person’s will;

children.label: B
children.text: the carnal knowledge, oral sodomy, sexual assault with an object, or sexual fondling of a person not forcibly or against the person’s will, where the victim is incapable of giving consent because of his or her youth or his or her temporary or permanent mental or physical incapacity; or

children.label: C
children.text: the carnal knowledge, oral sodomy, sexual assault with an object, or sexual fondling of a person achieved through the exploitation of the fear or threat of physical violence or bodily injury.

children.heading: Sexual assault with an object
children.label: 10 Sexual assault with an object
children.text: The term “sexual assault with an object” means the use of any hand, finger, object, or other instrument to penetrate, however slightly, the genital or anal opening of the body of another person.

children.heading: Sexual fondling
children.label: 11 Sexual fondling
children.text: The term “sexual fondling” means the touching of the private body parts of another person (including the genitalia, anus, groin, breast, inner thigh, or buttocks) for the purpose of sexual gratification.

children.label: A
children.text: custodial or medical personnel gathering physical evidence, or engaged in other legitimate medical treatment, in the course of investigating prison rape;

children.label: B
children.text: the use of a health care provider’s hands or fingers or the use of medical devices in the course of appropriate medical treatment unrelated to prison rape; or

children.label: C
children.text: the use of a health care provider’s hands or fingers and the use of instruments to perform body cavity searches in order to maintain security and safety within the prison or detention facility, provided that the search is conducted in a manner consistent with constitutional requirements.
